cavalier.name: Cavalier
description: ## Cavalier
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/32›

While many warriors strive to perfect their art, spending all of their time honing their skill at martial arms, others spend as much effort dedicating themselves to a cause. These warriors, known as cavaliers, swear themselves to a purpose, serving it above all else. Cavaliers are skilled at fighting from horseback, and are often found charging across a battlefield, with the symbol of their order trailing on a long, fluttering banner. The cavalier's true power comes from the conviction of his ideals, the oaths that he swears, and the challenges he makes.

**Role:** Cavaliers tend to marshal forces on the battlefield, using their mounted talents and challenges to control the flow of the fight. Outside of battle, cavaliers can be found advancing their cause through diplomacy and, if needed, subterfuge. The cavalier is no stranger to courtly intrigue and can hold his own in even the most delicate of social situations.
::class{any hd=10 wealth=5 skp=4 bluff climb craft diplo ha intm prof ride sm swim}
### Class Features
| Level | Base Attack Bonus | Fort Save | Ref Save | Will Save | Special |
| 1st | +1 | +2 | +0 | +0 | Challenge 1/day, mount, order, tactician |
| 2nd | +2 | +3 | +0 | +0 | Order ability |
| 3rd | +3 | +3 | +1 | +1 | Cavalier's charge |
| 4th | +4 | +4 | +1 | +1 | Challenge 2/day, expert trainer |
| 7th | +7/+2 | +5 | +2 | +2 | Challenge 3/day |
| 8th | +8/+3 | +6 | +2 | +2 | Order ability |
| 9th | +9/+4 | +6 | +3 | +3 | Greater tactician |
| 12th | +12/+7/+2 | +8 | +4 | +4 | Bonus feat, demanding challenge |
::prof{simple martial heavy light medium shields}

::ab[Challenge (Ex)]{jl=strip icon=melee l=1 usage="Once a day." swift="You challenge a foe to combat, choosing one target within sight to challenge. Your melee attacks deal extra damage whenever the attacks are made against the target of your challenge. This extra damage is equal to your level. Challenging a foe requires much of your concentration. You take a -2 penalty to your Armor Class, except against attacks made by the target of your challenge." special="The challenge remains in effect until the target is dead or unconscious or until the combat ends. Each challenge also includes another effect which is listed in the section describing your *order.*" imp4="You can *challenge* twice a day." imp7="You can *challenge* 3 times a day." imp10="You can *challenge* 4 times a day." imp13="You can *challenge* 5 times a day." imp16="You can *challenge* 6 times a day." imp19="You can *challenge* 7 times a day."}

:::ab{jl=strip icon=power l=1 action=Ability title="Mount (Ex)" special="Your bond with your mount is strong, with the two of you learning to anticipate each other's moods and moves. Should your mount die, you may find another mount to serve you after 1 week of mourning. This new mount does not gain the *link, evasion, devotion,* or *improved evasion* special abilities until the next time you gain a level."}

You gain the service of a loyal and trusty steed to carry you into battle. This mount functions as a druid's ‹sidekick/animal companion›, using your level as your effective druid level. The creature must be one that you are capable of riding and is suitable as a mount. This mount may come from the following list:

**Medium Cavaliers:** ‹companion/Axe Beak›, ‹companion/Camel›, ‹companion/Elk›, ‹companion/Giraffe›, ‹companion/Horse›, ‹companion/Giant Seahorse›, ‹companion/Giant Tortoise›, ‹companion/Zebra›; **Small Cavaliers:** ‹companion/Antelope›, ‹companion/Boar› (requires 4th level), ‹companion/Capybara›, ‹companion/Dog› (requires 4th level), ‹companion/Kangaroo›, ‹companion/Giant Gecko›, ‹companion/Pony›, ‹companion/Ram›, Reindeer (uses elk statistics), ‹companion/Stag›, ‹companion/Giant Weasel›, ‹companion/Wolf›, ‹companion/Wolfdog›
The GM might approve other animals as suitable mounts.

You do not take an armor check penalty on Ride checks while riding your mount. The mount is always considered combat trained and begins play with ‹feat/Light Armor Proficiency› as a bonus feat. Your mount does not gain the *share spells* special ability.

::ab[Order (Ex):]{jl=strip l=1 icon=boost ability="You must pledge yourself to a specific ‹ability/order<s›. The order grants you a number of bonuses, class skills, and special abilities. In addition, each order includes a number of edicts that you must follow. If you violate any of these edicts, you lose the benefits from your order's *challenge* ability for 24 hours. The violation of an edict is subject to GM interpretation." special="You cannot change your order without undertaking a lengthy process to dedicate yourself to a new cause. When this choice is made, you immediately lose all of the benefits from your old order. You must then follow the edicts of your new order for one entire level without gaining any benefits from that order. Once accomplished, you gain all of the bonuses from your new order. (Note that the names of these orders might vary depending upon the campaign setting or GM's preference.)"}

::ab[Tactician (Ex)]{jl=strip l=1 icon=power-boost order=asu ability="You receive a ‹main/teamwork feat› as a bonus feat. You must meet the prerequisites for this feat." standard="You can grant this feat to all allies within 30 feet who can see and hear you. Allies retain the use of this bonus feat for 3 rounds plus 1 round for every two levels you possess. Allies do not need to meet the prerequisites of these bonus feats." useInc="5~fighter~+" useF="1~1~5~~5"}

::ab[Cavalier's Charge (Ex)]{jl=strip l=3 icon=melee-def passive="You receive a +4 bonus on melee attack rolls on a ‹rule/charge› while mounted (instead of the normal +2). In addition, you do not suffer any penalty to your AC after making a charge attack while mounted."}

::ab[Expert Trainer (Ex)]{jl=strip l=4 icon=boost passive="You receive a bonus equal to ‹misc/half› your cavalier level whenever you use ‹skill/Handle Animal› on an animal that serves as a mount. In addition, you can reduce the time needed to teach a mount a new ‹ability/animal_>trick<s› or train a mount for a general purpose to 1 day per 1 week required by increasing the DC by +5. You can also train more than one mount at once, although each mount after the first adds +2 to the DC."}

::ab[Banner (Ex)]{jl=strip l=5 flavor="A cavalier's banner becomes a symbol of inspiration to his allies and companions." icon=def-melee passive="As long as your banner is clearly visible, all allies within 60 feet receive a +2 morale bonus on saving throws against fear and a +1 morale bonus on attack rolls made as part of a charge. The save bonus is equal to 1 + one-fifth your cavalier level, while the charge bonus is equal to one-fifth your cavalier level." special="The banner must be at least Small or larger and must be carried or displayed by you or your mount to function."}
:::aside

Some knights inspire and direct their allies with specialized tactics. A cavalier can select [a different battle standard](ability/banners) upon gaining the banner class feature, and its benefits replace the normal benefits of the banner and greater banner class features.

::ab[Bonus Feat]{jl=strip l=6 icon=power ability="You gain a bonus feat in addition to those gained from normal advancement. This bonus feat must be selected from those listed as ‹main/combat feat«s›. You must meet the prerequisites of this bonus feat." repeatAt="You gain another bonus feat, following the restrictions above.~12~18"}

::ab[Greater Tactician (Ex)]{jl="Other Abilities" l=9 icon=power order=aw ability="You receive an additional teamwork feat as a bonus feat. You must meet the prerequisites for this feat. You can grant this feat to your allies using the *tactician* ability." swift="Using the *tactician* ability becomes a @HLswift_action."}

::ab[Mighty Charge (Ex)]{l=11 flavor="A cavalier learns to make devastating charge attacks while mounted." passive="You double the threat range of any weapons wielded during a charge while mounted. This increase does not stack with other effects that increase the threat range of the weapon." free="In addition, you can make a free ‹rule/bull rush›, ‹rule/disarm›, ‹rule/sunder›, or ‹rule/trip› combat maneuver if your charge attack is successful. This does not provoke an attack of opportunity." order=pf icon=melee-power}

::ab[Demanding Challenge (Ex)]{l=12 icon=lower ability="Whenever you declare a challenge, your target must pay attention to the threat you pose. As long as the target is within your threatened area, it takes a -2 penalty to its AC from attacks made by anyone other than you."}

::ab[Greater Banner (Ex)]{l=14 flavor="A cavlier's banner becomes a rallying call to his allies." icon=def passive="All allies within 60 feet receive a +2 morale bonus on saving throws against charm and compulsion spells and effects." standard="While your banner is displayed, you can wave the banner through the air, granting all allies within 60 feet an additional saving throw against any one spell or effect that is targeting them. This save is made at the original DC. Spells and effects that do not allow saving throws are unaffected by this ability. An ally cannot benefit from this ability more than once per day." order=ps}

::ab[Master Tactician (Ex)]{l=17 icon=power ability="You receive an additional teamwork feat as a bonus feat. You must meet the prerequisites for this feat. You can grant this feat to your allies using the *tactician* ability." special="Whenever you use the *tactician* ability, you grant any two teamwork feats that you know. You can select from **any** of your teamwork feats, not just your bonus feats."}

::ab[Supreme Charge (Ex)]{l=20 icon=melee passive="Whenever you make a charge attack while mounted, you deal double the normal amount of damage (or triple if using a ‹eq-weapon/lance›). In addition, if you confirm a critical hit on a charge attack while mounted, the target is ‹misc/stunned› for 1d4 rounds. A Will save reduces this to ‹misc/staggered› for 1d4 rounds. The DC is equal to 10 + your base attack bonus."}
::h3[Alternate Capstones]{jl}
‹SOURCE Chronicle of Legends/28›
@altCapstoneDesc

::altCapstone[Tactical Genius (Ex)]{greatBeast capstone="At 20th level, the cavalier is a maestro of the battlefield, commanding and moving troops like so many game pieces. The cavalier gains a bonus teamwork feat, can grant up to three bonus feats with tactician, and increases the range of tactician by 90 feet."}
::h3[Favored Class Bonuses]{jl}
:::div{className="hanging striped"}
**Aasimar:** Add +1/4 to the cavalier's bonus on damage against targets of his challenge.[^A]
**Aquatic elf:** Add 1 skill rank to the cavalier's mount. If the cavalier ever replaces his mount, the new mount gains these bonus skill ranks.[^B]
**Catfolk:** Add +1/4 to the cavalier's banner bonus.[^C]
**Dwarf:** Add +1/2 to the cavalier's bonus to damage against targets of his challenge.[^D]
**Elf:** Add +1 hit point to the cavalier's mount. If the cavalier ever replaces his mount, the new mount gains these bonus hit points.[^E] [^F]
**Gathlain:** Add a +1/2 bonus to the cavalier's Knowledge (nature) and Knowledge (nobility) checks relating to fey courts and fey courtiers.[^G]

**Gnome:** Add +1 to the cavalier's mounted base speed. In combat this has no effect unless the cavalier has selected this reward five times (or another increment of five). If the cavalier ever replaces his mount, the new mount gains this bonus to its speed.[^H]
**Goblin:** Add +1 hit points to the cavalier's mount companion. If the cavalier ever replaces his mount, the new mount gains these bonus hit points.[^I] [^J]

**Half-elf:** Add +1 foot to the cavalier's mount's base speed. This option has no effect unless the cavalier has selected it 5 times (or another increment of 5); a speed of 54 feet is effectively a speed of 50 feet, for example. If the cavalier ever replaces his mount, the new companion gains this base speed bonus.[^K]
**Halfling:** Add +1/2 to the cavalier's effective class level for the purposes of determining the damage he deals when making an attack of opportunity against a challenged foe.[^L]
**Halfling:** The cavalier's mount gains a +1/2 bonus on saving throws against fear effects. If the cavalier ever replaces his mount, the new mount gains this bonus.[^M]
**Half-orc:** Add +1 hit point to the cavalier's mount companion. If the cavalier ever replaces his mount, the new mount gains these bonus hit points.[^N]
**Hobgoblin:** Add a +1/2 bonus on Intimidate checks and Ride checks.[^O]
**Human:** Add +1/4 to the cavalier's banner bonus.[^P] [^Q]
**Kitsune:** Gain 1/6 of a new Magical Tail feat. Any kitsune character can choose this bonus upon gaining a level in her favored class.[^R]
**Kitsune:** Add 1/4 to the cavalier's banner bonus.[^R]
**Kobold:** Add 5 feet (up to 15 feet maximum) to the cavalier's mount's speed when it uses the charge or withdraw action.[^S]
**Locathah:** Add 1 hit point to the cavalier's mount. If the cavalier ever replaces his mount, the new mount gains these bonus hit points.[^T]

**Nagaji:** Add 1 foot to the base speed of the cavalier's mount. This has no effect in combat unless the cavalier has selected this reward five times (or another increment of five). If the cavalier ever replaces this mount, the new mount gains this bonus to its speed.[^U]
**Orc:** Add +1 to the cavalier's CMB when making bull rush or overrun combat maneuvers against a challenged target.[^V]
**Vanara:** Add 1/2 to the cavalier's level when determining the duration of the tactician ability.[^W]
::h3[Archetypes]{jl}

::archetype[Beast Rider]{c=cavalier r="Mount; Expert Trainer" e="Where some cavaliers are simply skilled with horses or well-trained knights, the beast rider spends his life in constant pursuit of the most perfect mount, forming bonds with greater, more powerful, and more exotic creatures."}

::archetype[Castellan]{c=cavalier r="Mount; Cavalier's Charge; Expert Trainer; Mighty Charge; Demanding Challenge; Supreme Charge" e="A castellan is a cavalier who dedicates himself to the defense of a castle or walled city."}

::archetype[Charger]{c=cavalier r="Class Skills; Weapon/Armor Proficiencies; Mount; Cavalier's Charge; Expert Trainer; Mighty Charge" e="(Centaur Only) No warrior better epitomizes the centaur's devastating martial power than the charger."}

::archetype[Circuit Judge]{c=cavalier r="Tactician; Greater Tactician; Master Tactician; Challenge; Demanding Challenge" e="A circuit judge claims a specific region, becoming an expert in the local laws and, perhaps, becoming a fearsome executioner."}

::archetype[Constable]{c=cavalier r="Class Skills; Mount; Cavalier's Charge; Expert Trainer; Banner; Mighty Charge; Supreme Charge; Greater Banner" e="Constables keep order in the narrow streets and dark alleys of settlements."}
::archetype[Courtly Knight]{c=cavalier r="Tactician; Greater Tactician; Master Tactician" e="These courtly knights are equally at home in a duel with words or swords."}

::archetype[Daring Champion]{c=cavalier r="Weapon/Armor Proficiencies; Mount; Cavalier's Charge; Expert Trainer; Mighty Charge; Supreme Charge" e="While many cavaliers are the champions of old fighting forms, some younger, more daring cavaliers mix a martial style influenced by the lighter armored and more flamboyant swashbuckler forms with the dedication of cavalier orders."}
::archetype[Daring General]{c=cavalier r="Bonus Feats; Demanding Challenge; Supreme Charge" e="Those cavaliers most gifted at command often become daring generals."}

::archetype[Disciple of the Pike]{c=cavalier r="Mount; Cavalier's Charge; Expert Trainer; Banner; Greater Banner; Mighty Charge; Supreme Charge" e="The Hellknight Order of the Pike are renowned monster hunters, taking on large game using ancient weapon arts combined with modern equipment."}

::archetype[Drakerider]{c=cavalier r="Mount; Tactician; Expert Trainer; Banner; Greater Tactician; Greater Banner; Master Tactician" e="While many cavaliers dream of riding a dragon into battle, drakeriders come to learn that the reality involves far more effort than they had expected."}

::archetype[Emissary]{c=cavalier r="Tactician; Banner; Greater Tactician; Greater Banner; Supreme Charge" e="Some cavaliers focus more on speed and mobility than they do on the raw power of the mounted charge."}

::archetype[Esquire]{c=cavalier r="Mount; Cavalier's Charge; Expert Trainer; Banner; Mighty Charge; Supreme Charge" e="The esquire is a roving knight who acquires the services of a faithful follower - an aide-de-camp - who serves as the esquire's assistant, spear carrier, and guard."}

::archetype[Fell Rider]{c=cavalier r="Mount; Cavalier's Charge; Expert Trainer; Banner; Mighty Charge; Greater Banner; Supreme Charge" e="(Hobgoblin Only) The fell rider rides a bestial steed, a mount mastered by him alone. He tramples his enemies down, leaving twisted bodies in his wake, and fear rides with him."}

::archetype[First Mother's Fang]{c=cavalier r="Class Skills; Weapon Proficiencies; Mount; Order" e="A first mother's fang acts as a servant of his nation, a skilled general and tactician in times of war and a noble governor during times of peace."}

::archetype[Gallant]{c=cavalier r="Order; Banner; Greater Banner" e="Gallants embody the virtues of honor, generosity, and civility. Their personal symbol serves as an inspiration to others in a courtly setting as much as on a battlefield."}

::archetype[Gendarme]{c=cavalier r="Tactician; Greater Tactician; Master Tactician; Supreme Charge" e="The gendarme cares less for the finer points of tactical precision than he does for the exhilaration of the charge: the rush of wind through the visor of his helmet, the feel of his couched lance, the satisfying shriek of armor giving way before his weapon's force as the point drives past metal into his foes."}

::archetype[Ghost Rider]{c=cavalier r="Mount; Tactician; Greater Tactician; Master Tactician; Cavalier's Charge; Expert Trainer; Banner; Mighty Charge; Greater Banner" e="Ghost riders are conflicted warriors who use the powers of the phantasmal dead even while seeking out and banishing the corruption of undeath from plagued communities."}

::archetype[Green Knight]{c=cavalier r="Tactician; Mount; Order; Cavalier's Charge; Expert Trainer; Banner; Greater Tactician; Mighty Charge; Greater Banner; Master Tactician; Supreme Charge" e="Some knights serve nature itself and can be found protecting unspoiled natural places or serving fey beings."}
::archetype[Herald Squire]{c=cavalier r="Tactician; 2nd-level Order Ability; Cavalier's Charge" e="The herald squire is the eyes and ears of a traveling knight."}

::archetype[Honor Guard]{c=cavalier r="Challenge; Cavalier's Charge; Mighty Charge; Demanding Challenge" e="Certain cavaliers are trained not as advance combatants, but as loyal guards, standing as firm defenders in the face of threats to their chosen charge."}

::archetype[Hooded Knight]{c=cavalier r="Class Skills; Mount; Tactician; Greater Tactician; Master Tactician" e="Some cavaliers wander the lonely roads of the world - any world, including the First World - protecting travelers and acting as their honor guard."}

::archetype[Huntmaster]{c=cavalier r="Weapon/Armor Proficiency; Challenge; Mount; Tactician; Greater Tactician; Master Tactician; Cavalier's Charge; Expert Trainer; Banner; Bonus Feats; Mighty Charge; Greater Banner; Supreme Charge" e="Huntmasters train the beasts favored by lordly castes into swift and deadly trackers."}

::archetype[Hussar]{c=cavalier r="Armor Proficiencies; Class Skills; Tactician; Banner; Greater Banner; Bonus Feats; Greater Tactician; Master Tactician" e="Agile and maneuverable cavalry, hussars make up for their relatively light armor with their speed and cunning."}

::archetype[Knight of Arnisant]{c=cavalier r="Tactician; Expert Trainer; Banner; Greater Tactician; Demanding Challenge; Greater Tactician" e="The cavaliers known as knights of Arnisant revere the famous general who was crucial in the defeat of the Whispering Tyrant, the powerful wizard-king. Knights of Arnisant call upon Arnisant to grant them protection from evil magic, just as the Shield of Aroden protected him."}

::archetype[Luring Cavalier]{c=cavalier r="Challenge; Cavalier's Charge; Mighty Charge; Demanding Challenge; Supreme Charge" e="Those who study the perfection of strategy and tactics know that picking the battlefield can grant advantages that only overwhelming numbers of allies can eclipse."}
::archetype[Musketeer]{c=cavalier r="Mount; Expert Trainer" e="Some cavaliers are entrusted by their masters with the care and use of expensive and powerful oddities - firearms."}
::archetype[Oceanrider]{c=cavalier r="Armor Proficiency; Mount; Expert Trainer" e="Oceanriders are underwater cavaliers who ride dolphins, orcas, or seahorses."}

::archetype[Qabarat Outrider]{c=cavalier r="Armor Proficiency; Tactician; Greater Tactician; Master Tactician; Banner; Greater Banner" e="(Lashunta Only) The war colleges of the Castrovelian city-state of Qabarat produce a distinctive breed of cavalier known as the Qabarat outrider. In defense of the lashunta city-states, outriders lead small units of regular troops, the members of which can link minds with one another to form bonds in battle few can match."}

::archetype[Qadiran Horselord]{c=cavalier r="Weapon and Armor Proficiency; Mount; Tactician; Cavalier's Charge; 6th-level Bonus Feat; Greater Tactician; Master Tactician" e="Much like the breed of horses they ride, Qadiran horselords pride themselves on their mobility and endurance."}

::archetype[Saurian Champion]{c=cavalier r="Class Skills; Weapon/Armor Proficiencies; Challenge; Order; Mount; Tactician; Greater Tactician; Master Tactician; Banner; Greater Banner" e="These primeval cavaliers idolize dinosaurs as apex predators and value them as powerful, oversized mounts."}

::archetype[Sister-in-Arms]{c=cavalier r="Challenge; Order; Mount; Cavalier's Charge; Expert Trainer; Mighty Charge; Supreme Charge" e="Some of the most charismatic Gray Maidens combine their devotion to their sisters with the intense tactical training they received, learning to perfectly direct their companions and inspiring them to survive and emerge victorious."}

::archetype[Spellscar Drifter]{c=cavalier r="Weapon/Armor Proficiency; Tactician; Cavalier's Charge; Banner; Bonus Feats; Greater Tactician; Mighty Charge; Demanding Challenge; Master Tactician; Supreme Charge" e="Spellscar drifters are self-reliant and always keep their firearms loaded - in the Spellscar Desert, a rider with a slow draw doesn't live long."}

::archetype[Standard Bearer]{c=cavalier r="Mount; Banner; Mighty Charge; Supreme Charge" e="Some cavaliers prefer to stand away from the fray, their banners a beacon shining brightly over the battlefield, rallying their troops to victory."}

::archetype[Strategist]{c=cavalier r="Expert Trainer; Greater Banner; 18th-level Bonus Feat" e="Some cavaliers make a lifelong mission out of their exceptional ability to direct troops on the battlefield, combining tactical insight with a preternatural skill at improving the teamwork of their allies in order to win the day."}

::archetype[Verdivant]{c=cavalier r="Expert Trainer; Tactician; Banner; Greater Tactician; Greater Banner; Master Tactician; Bonus Feats; Cavalier's Charge" e="Some vine leshys' spirits are vibrant enough to project this animus beyond their own bodies"}

::archetype[Vermin Tamer]{c=cavalier r="Mount; Expert Trainer; Demanding Challenge" e="Due to the strange terrain, the lack of sunlight, and numerous other hazards, keeping a traditional mount in the Darklands can prove difficult. Cavaliers native to or traveling the region may choose to tame local creatures instead."}

::archetype[Wave Rider]{c=cavalier r="Weapon/Armor Proficiency; Mount" e="Throughout the Inner Sea region, wave riders patrol harbors, keeping them safe from dangerous sea creatures and watching for smugglers. The most famous of these is the hippocampus-riding Sea Cavalry of Absalom."}

cleric.name: Cleric
description: ## Cleric
‹SOURCE PRPG Core Rulebook/38›

In faith and the miracles of the divine, many find a greater purpose. Called to serve powers beyond most mortal understanding, all priests preach wonders and provide for the spiritual needs of their people. Clerics are more than mere priests, though; these emissaries of the divine work the will of their deities through strength of arms and the magic of their gods. Devoted to the tenets of the religions and philosophies that inspire them, these ecclesiastics quest to spread the knowledge and influence of their faith. Yet while they might share similar abilities, clerics prove as different from one another as the divinities they serve, with some offering healing and redemption, others judging law and truth, and still others spreading conflict and corruption. The ways of the cleric are varied, yet all who tread these paths walk with the mightiest of allies and bear the arms of the gods themselves.

**Role:** More than capable of upholding the honor of their deities in battle, clerics often prove stalwart and capable combatants. Their true strength lies in their capability to draw upon the power of their deities, whether to increase their own and their allies' prowess in battle, to vex their foes with divine magic, or to lend healing to companions in need.

As their powers are influenced by their faith, all clerics must focus their worship upon a divine source. While the vast majority of clerics revere a specific deity, a small number dedicate themselves to a divine concept worthy of devotion - such as battle, death, justice, or knowledge - free of a deific abstraction. (Work with your GM if you prefer this path to selecting a specific deity.)

::class{al="A cleric's alignment must be within one step of her deity's, along either the law/chaos axis or the good/evil axis." noAlignmentTable hd=8 wealth=4 skp=2 app craft diplo heal ka kh kno kp kr ling prof sm spc}
### Class Features and Spells Per Day
| Level | Base Attack Bonus | Fort Save | Ref Save | Will Save | Special | 0 | 1st | 2nd | 3rd | 4th | 5th | 6th | 7th | 8th | 9th |
| 1st | +0 | +2 | +0 | +2 | Aura, channel energy 1d6, domains, orisons | 3 | 1+1 | - | - | - | - | - | - | - | - |
::prof{simple light medium shields wExtra="The favored weapon of their deity"}

::ab[Bonus Languages]{jl=strip icon=boost l=1 passive="Your bonus language options include Celestial, Abyssal, and Infernal (the languages of good, chaotic evil, and lawful evil outsiders, respectively). These choices are in addition to the bonus languages available to you because of your race."}

::ab[Aura (Ex)]{jl=strip icon=aura l=1 passive="If you are a cleric of a chaotic, evil, good, or lawful deity, you have a particularly powerful aura corresponding to your deity's alignment (see ‹spell/detect evil›)."}
::spellAb{caster=cleric alignLimit ritualDivine stat=Wisdom type=divine meditate choice prepare}
::cantrips{c=orisons p}

::ab[Chaotic, Evil, Good, and Lawful Spells]{jl="Aligned Spells" icon=magic passive="You can't cast spells of an alignment opposed to your own or your deity's (if you have one). Spells associated with particular alignments are indicated by the chaos, evil, good, and law descriptors in their spell descriptions."}

::ab[Channel Energy (Su)]{jl=strip l=1 icon=power-aura flavor="Regardless of alignment, any cleric can release a wave of energy by channeling the power of her faith through her holy (or unholy) symbol. This energy can be used to cause or heal damage, depending on the type of energy channeled and the creatures targeted." provokes=No standard="You must present your holy symbol to channel energy. This causes a ‹misc/burst› that affects all creatures of one type (either undead or living) in a 30-foot radius centered on you. This deals or heals (see below) 1d6 damage. You can choose whether or not to include yourself in this effect. Creatures that take damage from channeled energy receive a Will save to halve the damage, with a DC equal to 10 + ‹misc/half› your cleric level + your Charisma modifier." special="A good cleric (or one who worships a good deity) channels @HLpositive_energy and can choose to deal damage to undead creatures or to heal living creatures. An evil cleric (or one who worships an evil deity) channels @HLnegative_energy and can choose to deal damage to living creatures or to heal undead creatures. A neutral cleric who worships a neutral deity (or one who is not devoted to a particular deity) must choose whether she channels positive or negative energy. Once this choice is made, it cannot be reversed. *This decision also determines whether the cleric casts spontaneous* cure *or* inflict *spells (see* Spontaneous Casting *below).* Creatures healed by channeled energy cannot exceed their maximum hit point total - all excess healing is lost." useMod=Cha3 imp3="The damage becomes 2d6." imp5="The damage becomes 3d6." imp7="The damage becomes 4d6." imp9="The damage becomes 5d6." imp11="The damage becomes 6d6." imp13="The damage becomes 7d6." imp15="The damage becomes 8d6." imp17="The damage becomes 9d6." imp19="The damage becomes 10d6."}
:::aside

Some deities may endow their mortal servants with the power to channel energies in other ways that more closely mirror their particular focus. You may choose one of these ‹ability/variant channeling« abilities› instead.
:::ab{jl=strip title=Domains flavor="A cleric's deity influences her alignment, what magic she can perform, her values, and how others see her." l=1 icon=power-magic action=Choice}

You choose two ‹ability/domains› from among those belonging to your deity. You can select an alignment domain (‹domain/Chaos›, ‹domain/Evil›, ‹domain/Good›, or ‹domain/Law›) only if your alignment matches that domain. If you are not devoted to a particular deity, you still select two domains to represent your spiritual inclinations and abilities (subject to GM approval). The restriction on alignment domains still applies.

Each domain grants a number of @HL[domain powers], dependent upon your cleric level, as well as a number of bonus spells. You gain one @HL[domain spell slot] for each level of cleric spell you can cast, from 1st on up. Each day, you can prepare one of the spells from your two domains in that slot. If a domain spell is not on the cleric spell list, you can prepare it only in your domain spell slot. Domain spells cannot be used to cast spells spontaneously.
In addition, you gain the listed powers from both of your domains, if you are of a high enough level. Unless otherwise noted, using a domain power is a @HL[standard action].

::ab[Spontaneous Casting:]{jl=strip flavor="A good cleric (or a neutral cleric of a good deity) can channel stored spell energy into healing spells that she did not prepare ahead of time." l=1 icon=magic ability="You can &quot&lose&quot& any prepared spell that is not an orison or domain spell in order to cast any *cure* spell of the same spell level or lower. This is only true if you channel positive energy. If you channel negative energy, you can convert spells to *inflict* spells." special="A *cure spell* is any spell with &quot&cure&quot& in its name, while an *inflict spell* is one with &quot&inflict&quot& in its name. These spells are ‹main/spell_>group«ed here<ings›."}
::h3[Ex-Clerics]{jl}

A cleric who grossly violates the code of conduct required by her god loses all spells and class features, except for armor and shield proficiencies and proficiency with simple weapons. She cannot thereafter gain levels as a cleric of that god until she [atones](spell/atonement) for her deeds.[^Ex]
::h3[Alternate Capstones]{jl}
‹SOURCE Chronicle of Legends/28›
@altCapstoneDesc

::altCapstone[Proxy (Su)]{soulChannel capstone="At 20th level, the cleric forges a direct, personal connection to her deity. She knows instinctively whether her deity would approve or disapprove of a given course of action or idea (though deities rarely bother to form opinions about overly mundane or trivial events). In addition, she can select an additional domain from the list offered by her deity."}
::h3[Favored Class Bonuses]{jl}
:::div{className="hanging striped"}
**Aasimar:** Add +1/2 to damage when using positive energy against undead or using Alignment Channel to damage evil outsiders.[^A]
**Aphorite:** Add a +1/2 bonus on Knowledge (planes) checks relating to Axis and creatures with the lawful subtype.[^B]
**Dhampir:** Add +1 to the caster level of any channeling feat used to affect undead.[^C]

**Drow:** Select one domain power granted at 1st level that is normally usable a number of times per day equal to 3 + the cleric's Wisdom modifier. The cleric adds +1/2 to the number of uses per day of that domain power.[^D]
**Duergar:** Add a +1/2 bonus on checks made to craft magic items.[^E]
**Duskwalker:** Add 1/2 point of damage when using positive energy against undead.[^F]

**Dwarf:** Select one domain power granted at 1st level that is normally usable a number of times per day equal to 3 + the cleric's Wisdom modifier. The cleric adds +1/2 to the number of uses per day of that domain power.[^G] [^H]

**Elf:** Select one domain power granted at 1st level that is normally usable a number of times per day equal to 3 + the cleric's Wisdom modifier. The cleric adds +1/2 to the number of uses per day of that domain power.[^I]
**Fetchling:** Add 1/2 point to negative energy damage dealt by channeling energy and *inflict wounds* spells.[^J]
**Gnome:** Add +1/2 to the cleric's channeled energy total when healing creatures of the animal, fey, and plant types.[^K]
**Half-elf:** Add +1/3 to the amount of damage dealt or damage healed when the cleric uses channel energy.[^L]
**Half-elf:** Add a +1/4 bonus on caster level checks to remove afflictions (curses, diseases, poisons, etc.).[^M]

**Halfling:** Select one domain power granted at 1st level that is normally usable a number of times per day equal to 3 + the cleric's Wisdom modifier. The cleric adds +1/2 to the number of uses per day of that domain power.[^N] [^O]

**Half-orc:** Select one domain power granted at 1st level that is normally usable a number of times per day equal to 3 + the cleric's Wisdom modifier. The cleric adds +1/2 to the number of uses per day of that domain power.[^P]
**Hobgoblin:** Add +1/2 to negative energy spell damage, including inflict spells.[^Q]
**Human:** Add a +1 bonus on caster level checks made to overcome the spell resistance of outsiders.[^R] [^S]
**Ifrit:** Add a +1/2 bonus on Knowledge (planes) checks relating to the Plane of Fire and creatures with the fire subtype.[^T]
**Kitsune:** Gain 1/6 of a new Magical Tail feat. Any kitsune character can choose this bonus upon gaining a level in her favored class.[^U]
**Kobold:** Add +1 to channel energy damage dealt to creatures denied their Dexterity bonus to AC (whether or not the creature has a Dexterity bonus to AC).[^V]
**Oread:** Add a +1/2 bonus on Knowledge (planes) checks relating to the Plane of Earth and creatures with the earth subtype.[^W]
**Sylph:** Add a +1/2 bonus on Knowledge (planes) checks relating to the Plane of Air and creatures with the air subtype.[^X]
**Tengu:** Gain a +1/2 bonus on Diplomacy checks to gather information and on Knowledge (local and religion) checks to know local religious practices and superstitions.[^Y]
**Tiefling:** Add a +1 bonus on caster level checks made to overcome the spell resistance of outsiders.[^Z]
**Undine:** Add a +1 bonus on caster level checks to overcome the spell resistance of creatures with the aquatic or water subtype.[^1]

**Wayang:** Add a +1/2 bonus to damage rolls the cleric makes when using channel energy and casting spells that deal negative energy damage or positive energy damage, including cure and inflict spells. This bonus does not apply to healing via negative energy effects or positive energy effects.[^2]
**Wyrwood:** Add 1/4 to the cleric's caster level when casting spells or using abilities from the Law domain or one of its subdomains, or when casting spells with the lawful descriptor.[^3]
::h3[Archetypes]{jl}

::archetype[Angelfire Apostle]{c=cleric r="Armor Proficiency; Spellcasting; Alignment; Channel Energy" e="Angelfire apostles use the powers of good to avoid violence when possible and cleanse both maladies and evil creatures with blinding flames."}

::archetype[Appeaser]{c=cleric r="Aura; Channel Energy; Alignment; Spells; Domains" e="A rare few seek the power of dark forces for brighter ends by focusing on the utilitarian aspects of a god's portfolio, appeasing their deities through high praise and glorifying their chosen gods' unaligned aspects."}
::archetype[Asmodean Advocate]{c=cleric r=Domains e="For the faithful of Asmodeus, the words used in a negotiation or contract matter more than their intent."}
::archetype[Blossoming Light]{c=cleric r="Armor Proficiency; Alignment; Channel Energy; Domains" e="Blossoming lights are clerics who take their pursuit of purity and light to extremes."}

::archetype[Cardinal]{c=cleric r="Armor Proficiency; Spontaneous Casting; Domain; Class Skills; Skill Ranks; Base Attack Bonus" e="Cardinals wield significant political power, engaging in the game of intrigue on behalf of their churches"}

::archetype[Channeler of the Unknown]{c=cleric r="Weapon and Armor Proficiency; Spells; Aura; Channel Energy; Domains; Spontaneous Casting" e="(Ex-cleric archetype) Channelers of the unknown are those faithless clerics who channel the power of an unknown entity or force of the universe"}

::archetype[Cloistered Cleric]{c=cleric r="Weapon/Armor Proficiencies; Domains; Spells" e="Cloistered clerics typically live in a temple and rarely interact with the outside world. They are bookish and well learned in the lore of the faith, paying less attention to its magical and martial aspects."}

::archetype[Crashing Wave]{c=cleric r="Deity; Bonus Languages; Channel Energy; Spontaneous Casting" e="Clerics who live in or near the sea sometimes embrace the watery aspect of Gozreh over the deity's other dimensions and take the title of crashing wave; such clerics understand the tempestuousness and cruelty of the sea, but also its generosity and bounty"}

::archetype[Crusader]{c=cleric r="Domains; Spells" e="Crusaders serve the militant arm of a church, ready to stand guard over the religion's holy places and to be its swift, avenging arm against those who resist its truth."}

::archetype[Demonic Apostle]{c=cleric r="Domains; Channel Energy" e="(Drow Only) Demon worship is common among the drow, and so are ranks of demonic apostles, who gain magical insight from their dark lords and crush their chaotic masters' enemies by channeling demonic energy."}
::archetype[Divine Paragon]{c=cleric r="Domain; Aura" e="Divine paragons strive to emulate their god's ideals as closely as possible."}

::archetype[Divine Scourge]{c=cleric r="Domains; Channel Energy" e="Divine scourges take on the role of dealing out unique punishments on behalf of their deities, taking pleasure in carrying out their sacrosanct duties."}

::archetype[Divine Strategist]{c=cleric r="Domains; Channel Energy" e="The divine strategist leads the armies of the faithful, not from the front lines but through her clever strategy and tactical acumen."}

::archetype[Ecclesitheurge]{c=cleric r="Weapon/Armor Proficiencies; Domain; Channel Energy" e="Eschewing physical armor for protection via the strength of his faith, an ecclesitheurge focuses on the miracles his deity bestows and the breadth of that deity's dominion."}

::archetype[Elder Mythos Cultist]{c=cleric r="Alignment; Domains; Channel Energy; Spontaneous Casting; 5th, 11th, and 19th-level Channel Energy increase" e="Typically grasping, secretive, and thoroughly mad, Elder Mythos cultists open their bodies and minds to horrifying realities not meant for the sane as they strive to prepare the world for the eventual return of their alien masters."}
::archetype[Evangelist]{c=cleric r="Domains; Channel Energy; Spontaneous Casting" e="The evangelist is the voice of her religion in the world."}

::archetype[Fiendish Vessel]{c=cleric r="Domains; Channel Energy" e="(Tiefling Only) Fiendish vessels, through their fiendish heritage, share an innate connection with their patron, and that connection grants them understanding and power."}

::archetype[Forgemaster]{c=cleric r="Domains; Channel Energy" e="(Dwarf Only) Forgemasters are priestly dwarves who are ritual casters and expert enchanters, able to produce their rune-graven armaments with astonishing speed."}

::archetype[Foundation of Faith]{c=cleric r="Channel Energy" e="Whether aiding the faithful or defending against the depredations of unbelievers, a foundation of faith is unshakable in her convictions."}

::archetype[Herald Caller]{c=cleric r="Class Skills; Domains; Armor Proficiencies" e="Unlike warpriests or paladins, who charge headlong into battle in the name of their patron deities, herald callers are adept at calling powerful outsiders to aid their brethren in battle."}

::archetype[Hidden Priest]{c=cleric r="1st-level Domain Power, 8th-level Domain Power" e="When practicing their religion is outlawed, the hidden priest learns to hide their true nature and practice magic in secret."}
::archetype[Idealist]{c=cleric r="Spontaneous Casting; Channel Energy" e="Idealist clerics are personifications of belief, each embracing the purest ideal of his deity's realm."}

::archetype[Iron Priest]{c=cleric r="Class Skills; Channel Energy; Spellcasting" e="Iron priests preach of the messengers from beyond the stars, envoys of the divine brought to Golarion in a falling star."}
::archetype[Lawspeaker]{c=cleric r="Alignment; Domains" e="Lawspeakers spread their philosophy of certain and unwavering justice throughout the Inner Sea region."}
::archetype[Mendevian Priest]{c=cleric r=Domains e="Crusade-minded clerics of Iomedae, Gorum, and other churches come to Mendev to learn battlefield tactics and the weaknesses of demons."}
::archetype[Merciful Healer]{c=cleric r="Domains; Channel Energy" e="The merciful healer is a master of battlefield revivification, sustaining and restoring allies to keep them in the fight."}

::archetype[Roaming Exorcist]{c=cleric r="Skill Ranks; Weapon/Armor Proficiencies; Domains; Channel Energy" e="The roaming exorcist travels far and wide to root out possessions, hauntings, and hidden evils. The roaming exorcist extracts unruly spirits from not only victims of possession, but also haunted sites and accursed items."}

::archetype[Sacred Attendant]{c=cleric r="Armor Proficiency; Channel Energy; Domains" e="Sacred attendants bring out the varied beauty in everyone they help, and they typically worship deities of beauty, love, and sex."}

::archetype[Scroll Scholar]{c=cleric r="1st Level Domain Power, Channel Energy 5th level increase, 4th-level spell slot" e="Those who trade some of their potential to better understand ancient texts and scrolls can become learned scroll scholars."}
::archetype[Separatist]{c=cleric r=Domains e="A radical cleric, unsatisfied with the orthodoxy of her deity's teachings, forges her own path of defiant divine expression."}
::archetype[Stoic Caregiver]{c=cleric r="Channel Energy; Domains" e="Stoic caregivers are champions of life in defiance of untimely death as well as undeath."}
::archetype[Theologian]{c=cleric r=Domains e="A theologian is an expert on one particular area of her religion"}

::archetype[Triadic Priest]{c=cleric r=Domains e="Power comes not just from communion with one's deity, but also from the formation of divine triumvirates. At the head of these groups are triadic priests."}
::archetype[Undead Lord]{c=cleric r=Domains e="An undead lord is a cleric focused on using necromancy to control undead."}

::archetype[Varisian Pilgrim]{c=cleric r="Altered Domain Abilities; Medium Armor/Shield Proficiency; 8th-level Domain Power" e="While most clerics are associated with a particular temple, adventuring clerics spend much of their time away from their favored place of worship, and there are those who worship primarily on the road and during the journey."}
