druid.name: Druid
description: ## Druid
‹SOURCE PRPG Core Rulebook/48›

Within the purity of the elements and the order of the wilds lingers a power beyond the marvels of civilization. Furtive yet undeniable, these primal magics are guarded over by servants of philosophical balance known as druids. Allies to beasts and manipulators of nature, these often misunderstood protectors of the wild strive to shield their lands from all who would threaten them and prove the might of the wilds to those who lock themselves behind city walls. Rewarded for their devotion with incredible powers, druids gain unparalleled shape-shifting abilities, the companionship of mighty beasts, and the power to call upon nature's wrath. The mightiest temper powers akin to storms, earthquakes, and volcanoes with primeval wisdom long abandoned and forgotten by civilization.

**Role:** While some druids might keep to the fringe of battle, allowing companions and summoned creatures to fight while they confound foes with the powers of nature, others transform into deadly beasts and savagely wade into combat. Druids worship personifications of elemental forces, natural powers, or nature itself. Typically this means devotion to a nature deity, though druids are just as likely to revere vague spirits, animalistic demigods, or even specific awe-inspiring natural wonders.
::class{al="Any neutral" ln ng ne n cn hd=8 wealth=2 skp=4 climb craft fly ha heal kg kna per prof ride spc sur swim}
### Class Features and Spells Per Day
| Level | Base Attack Bonus | Fort Save | Ref Save | Will Save | Special | 0 | 1st | 2nd | 3rd | 4th | 5th | 6th | 7th | 8th | 9th |
| 1st | +0 | +2 | +0 | +2 | Nature bond, nature sense, orisons, wild empathy | 3 | 1 | - | - | - | - | - | - | - | - |
| 4th | +3 | +4 | +1 | +4 | Resist nature's lure, wild shape (1/day) | 4 | 3 | 2 | - | - | - | - | - | - | - |

::prof{weaps=club~dagger~dart~quarterstaff~scimitar~scythe~sickle~shortspear~sling~spear wExtra="All natural attacks (claw, bite, and so forth) of any form the druid assumes with wild shape" light medium shields aExtra="Druids are prohibited from wearing metal armor or using metal shields, but is allowed to wear wooden armor that has been altered by the ‹spell/ironwood› spell so that it functions as though it were steel." extra="A druid who wears prohibited armor or uses a prohibited shield is unable to cast druid spells or use any of her supernatural or spell-like class abilities while doing so and for 24 hours thereafter."}
::spellAb{type=divine druid alignLimit prepare ritualDivine stat=Wisdom caster=druid}
::cantrips{c=orisons p}

::ab[Spontaneous Casting]{jl=strip flavor="A druid can channel stored spell energy into summoning spells that she hasn't prepared ahead of time." l=1 icon=magic ability="You can &quot&lose&quot& a prepared spell in order to cast any ‹spell/summon nature's ally› spell of the same level or lower."}

::ab[Chaotic, Evil, Good, and Lawful Spells]{jl="Aligned Spells" icon=magic passive="You can't cast spells of an alignment opposed to your own or your deity's (if you have one). Spells associated with particular alignments are indicated by the chaos, evil, good, and law descriptors in their spell descriptions."}

::ab[Bonus Languages]{jl=strip l=1 icon=boost passive="Your bonus language options include Sylvan, the language of woodland creatures. This choice is in addition to the bonus languages available to you because of your race. You also know Druidic, a secret language known only to druids, which you learn upon becoming a 1st-level druid." special="Druidic is a free language; that is, you know it in addition to your regular allotment of languages and it doesn't take up a language slot. Druids are forbidden to teach this language to non-druids. Druidic has its own alphabet."}
::ab[Nature Bond (Ex)]{jl=strip l=1 head icon=power choice="You form a bond with nature. This bond can take one of three forms: domain, companion, or herbalism."}

::ab[Domain]{sub icon=power-magic ability="You gain one of the following cleric domains: ‹domain/Air›, ‹domain/Animal›, ‹domain/Earth›, ‹domain/Fire›, ‹domain/Plant›, ‹domain/Water›, or ‹domain/Weather›. Alternately, you can choose a ‹ability/druid«-specific» domain<s›. When determining the powers and bonus spells granted by this domain, your effective cleric level is equal to your druid level. A druid that selects this option also receives additional domain spell slots, just like a ‹class/cleric›. You must prepare the spell from your domain in this slot and this spell cannot be used to cast a spell spontaneously."}

::ab[Companion]{sub icon=power ability="You may begin play with any of the animals listed in ‹ability/druid>Animal» Companions›, who becomes a loyal companion that accompanies you on your adventures. Unlike normal animals of its kind, an ‹sidekick/animal companion«'s› Hit Dice, abilities, skills, and feats advance as you advance in level." special="If you receive an animal companion from more than one source, your effective druid levels stack for the purposes of determining the statistics and abilities of the companion. Most animal companions increase in size when their druid reaches 4th or 7th level, depending on the companion. If you release your companion from service, you may gain a new one by performing a ceremony requiring 24 uninterrupted hours of prayer in the environment where the new companion typically lives. This ceremony can also replace an animal companion that has perished."}

::ab[Herbalism]{sub icon=power-magic order=nua note="You can use combinations of nuts, berries, dried herbs, and other natural ingredients along with appropriate containers to create herbal concoctions or magic consumables that function like potions. This acts like the ‹feat/Brew Potion› feat, but only for spells on the druid spell list. Herbal concoctions are typically thick and sludgy, and their creation time, caster level, spell duplication capabilities, and all other variables and properties are identical to those of potions created using Brew Potion. Herbal concoctions created with herbs that cause special effects when ingested retain those effects as well as the appropriate spell effect." useMod=Wis ability="You can create a free herbal concoction. Additional concoctions cost the same as creating an equivalent potion using Brew Potion. You can sell your concoctions just as if they were potions (though NPCs unfamiliar with druidic herbalism may need some convincing before purchasing these wares)." imp4="You can now make a concoction of any spell on any list that you can cast. Your additional concoctions take ‹misc/half› the time to make, at half the cost. You can also disguise the effects of a concoction. When a creature fails a Perception or Spellcraft check to identify one, the concoction appears to be a different herbal concoction of your choice. You must designate this false result when creating the concoction. If a creature exceeds the check's DC by 5 or more, it correctly identifies the concoction, though not that you tried to fool it." imp7="A creature must exceed the DC by 10 to correctly identify a disguised concoction. Also, you can create any herbal concoction in 1 minute. You can also create a special concoction of any spell higher than 3rd level that you can cast, but you must expend a spell slot of the same level. These special concoctions do not cost you anything to create and function like extracts created by an ‹class/alchemist› with the ‹discovery/infusion› discovery."}
::ab[Nature Sense (Ex)]{jl=strip icon=boost l=1 passive="You gain a +2 bonus on Knowledge (nature) and Survival checks."}

::ab[Wild Empathy (Ex)]{jl=strip icon=boost l=1 flavor="A druid can improve the attitude of an animal." ability="This ability functions just like a ‹skill/Diplomacy› check made to improve the attitude of a person. You roll 1d20 and add your druid level and your Charisma modifier to determine the wild empathy check result. The typical domestic animal has a starting attitude of indifferent, while wild animals are usually unfriendly. You can also use this ability to influence a magical beast with an Intelligence score of 1 or 2, but you take a -4 penalty on the check." special="To use wild empathy, you and the animal must be within 30 feet of one another under normal conditions. Generally, influencing an animal in this way takes 1 minute but, as with influencing people, it might take more or less time."}

::ab[Woodland Stride (Ex)]{jl=strip l=2 icon=boost passive="You may move through any sort of undergrowth (such as natural thorns, briars, overgrown areas, and similar terrain) at your normal speed and without taking damage or suffering any other impairment. Thorns, briars, and overgrown areas that have been magically manipulated to impede motion, however, still affect you."}
::ab[Trackless Step (Ex)]{jl=strip l=3 icon=boost passive="You leave no trail in natural surroundings and cannot be tracked. You may choose to leave a trail if so desired."}

::ab[Resist Nature's Lure (Ex)]{jl=strip l=4 icon=def passive="You gain a +4 bonus on saving throws against the spell-like and supernatural abilities of fey. This bonus also applies to spells and effects that utilize or target plants, such as ‹spell/blight›, ‹spell/entangle›, ‹spell/spike growth›, and ‹spell/warp wood›."}

::ab[Wild Shape (Su)]{jl=strip l=4 icon=power provokes=No usage="Once a day." standard="You gain the ability to turn yourself into any Small or Medium animal and back again. Your options for new forms include all creatures with the ‹main/monsters_>animal<s› type. This ability functions like the ‹spell/beast shape I› spell, except as noted here. The effect lasts for 1 hour per druid level, or until you change back. The form chosen must be that of an animal with which you are familiar." special="You lose your ability to speak while in animal form because you are limited to the sounds that a normal, untrained animal can make, but you can communicate normally with other animals of the same general grouping as your new form. (The normal sound a wild parrot makes is a squawk, so changing to this form does not permit speech.)" imp6="You can *wild shape* twice a day as ‹spell/beast shape II›, and you can now change into a Large or Tiny animal or a Small ‹main/monsters_outsiders_>elemental› (as ‹spell/elemental body I›)." imp8="You can *wild shape* 3 times/day as ‹spell/beast shape III›, and you can now change into a Huge or Diminutive animal, a Small or Medium elemental (as ‹spell/elemental body II›), or a Small or Medium ‹main/monsters_>plant<s› creature (as ‹spell/plant shape I›)." imp10="You can *wild shape* 4 times/day, and can choose to change into a Small, Medium, or Large elemental (as ‹spell/elemental body III›) or a Small, Medium, or Large plant creature (as ‹spell/plant shape II›)." imp12="You can *wild shape* 5 times/day, and can choose to change into a Small, Medium, Large, or Huge elemental (as ‹spell/elemental body IV›) or a Small, Medium, Large, or Huge plant creature (as ‹spell/plant shape III›)." imp14="You can *wild shape* 6 times/day." imp16="You can *wild shape* 7 times/day." imp18="You can *wild shape* 8 times/day." imp20="You can *wild shape* at will."}
::ab[Venom Immunity (Ex)]{jl="Other Abilities" l=9 icon=def passive="You have immunity to all poisons."}
::ab[A Thousand Faces (Su)]{l=13 icon=power standard="You can change your appearance at will, as if using the ‹spell/alter self› spell, but only while in your normal form."}

::ab[Timeless Body (Ex)]{l=15 icon=boost-def passive="You no longer take ability score penalties for aging and cannot be magically ‹rule/age«d›. Any penalties you may have already incurred, however, remain in place. Bonuses still accrue, and you still die of old age when your time is up."}
::h3[Ex-Druids]{jl}

A druid who ceases to revere nature, changes to a prohibited alignment, or teaches the Druidic language to a non-druid loses all spells and druid abilities (including her animal companion, but not including weapon, armor, and shield proficiencies). She cannot thereafter gain levels as a druid until she [atones](spell/atonement).[^Ex]
::h3[Alternate Capstones]{jl}
‹SOURCE Chronicle of Legends/28›
@altCapstoneDesc

::altCapstone[Home Ground (Su)]{greatBeast capstone="At 20th level, the druid knows every tree, every deer, every blade of grass, and every scuttling beetle of her home. The druid selects a specific area relevant to the campaign, such as a prominent forest, mountain, or even a patch of sea. The druid is constantly under the effect of ‹spell/commune with nature› with regards to that area and can change the facts she gleans from the spell when she prepares her spells each day. In addition, while on her home ground, the druid gains a +4 bonus to her caster level."}
::h3[Favored Class Bonuses]{jl}
:::div{className="hanging striped"}

**Aquatic elf:** Add 1 hit point to the druid's animal companion. If the druid ever replaces her animal companion, the new animal companion gains these bonus hit points. For a druid whose nature bond gives her a cleric domain, select one cleric domain power at 1st level that is normally usable a number of times per day equal to 3 + the druid's Wisdom modifier. The druid adds 1/2 to the number of uses per day of that domain power.[^A]
**Catfolk:** Add +1 hit points to the druid's animal companion. If the druid ever replaces her animal companion, the new animal companion gains these bonus hit points.[^B]
**Drow:** Add a +1 bonus to wild empathy checks to influence animals and magical beasts that live underground.[^C]

**Dwarf:** Select one domain power granted at 1st level that is normally usable a number of times per day equal to 3 + the druid's Wisdom modifier. The druid adds +1/2 to the number of uses per day of that domain power.[^D]
**Elf:** Add +1/3 to the druid's natural armor bonus when using wild shape.[^E]

**Fetchling:** The druid's animal companion gains resistance 1 against either cold or electricity. Each time the druid selects this reward, he increases his animal companion's resistance to one of those energy types by 1 (maximum 10 for any one energy type). If the druid ever replaces his animal companion, the new animal companion gains these resistances.[^F]
**Ganzi:** The druid gains a +1 bonus on Knowledge (nature) or Knowledge (planes) checks related to extraplanar flora and fauna.[^G]
**Gathlain:** Add a +1/2 bonus to wild empathy checks.[^H]
**Ghoran:** When casting spells that target plants or plant creatures, add 1/3 to the effective caster level, but only for the purpose of determining duration.[^I]

**Gnome:** The druid gains energy resistance 1 against acid, cold, electricity, or fire. Each time the druid selects this reward, increase her resistance to one of these energy types by +1 (maximum 10 for any one type).[^J] [^K]
**Gnome:** The druid's animal companion gains 1/4 point of DR/cold iron (maximum DR 5/cold iron). If the druid replaces her companion, the new companion gains this DR.[^L]
**Goblin:** Add +1 hit points to the druid's animal companion. If the druid ever replaces her animal companion, the new animal companion gains these bonus hit points.[^M] [^N]
**Grippli:** Add a +1/2 bonus on ‹rule/concentration› checks. This bonus doubles in a forest or swamp terrain.[^O]

**Half-elf:** Select one cleric domain power at 1st level that is normally usable a number of times per day equal to 3 + the druid's Wisdom modifier. The druid adds +1/2 to the number of uses per day of that domain power. For druids whose nature bond gives them an animal companion, add +1 skill rank to the animal companion. If the druid ever replaces her animal companion, the new companion gains these bonus skill ranks.[^P] [^Q]
**Halfling:** Add a +1/4 luck bonus on the saving throws of the druid's animal companion.[^R]
**Half-orc:** Add +1/3 to the druid's natural armor bonus when using wild shape.[^S]
**Human:** Add a +1/2 bonus on Diplomacy and Intimidate checks to change a creature's attitude.[^T] [^U]
**Kitsune:** Gain 1/6 of a new Magical Tail feat. Any kitsune character can choose this bonus upon gaining a level in her favored class.[^V]
**Kitsune:** Add a +1/2 bonus on Diplomacy and Intimidate checks to change a creature's attitude.[^W]
**Kobold:** Add +1/2 to the druid's wild empathy bonus.[^X]
**Locathah:** Add 1 skill rank to the druid's animal companion. If the druid ever replaces her animal companion, the new animal companion gains these bonus skill ranks.[^Y]
**Merfolk:** Add +1 hit point to the druid's animal companion. If the merfolk ever replaces her animal companion, the new animal companion gains these bonus hit points.[^Z]
**Orc:** Add +1/2 to the damage dealt by the druid's animal companion's natural attacks.[^1]
**Oread:** Add a +1/2 bonus on Knowledge (nature) checks relating to plants and burrowing animals.[^2]
**Ratfolk:** Add a +1 bonus on wild empathy checks made to influence animals and magical beasts that live underground.[^3]
**Strix:** Add a +1/2 bonus to Knowledge (nature) checks related to weather and flying animals.[^4]
**Sylph:** Add a +1/2 bonus on Knowledge (nature) checks relating to weather and flying animals.[^5]
**Tiefling:** Add a +1 bonus on wild empathy checks made to improve the attitude of fiendish animals.[^6]
**Undine:** Add a +1 bonus on wild empathy checks to influence animals and magical beasts with the aquatic subtype.[^7]
**Vanara:** Add a +1/2 bonus on wild empathy checks and a +1/2 bonus on Handle Animal skill checks.[^8]
**Vine leshy:** Add a +1/2 bonus to the druid's wild empathy or plant empathy checks.[^9]

**Wayang:** Select one domain power granted at 1st level that is normally usable a number of times per day equal to 3 + the druid's Wisdom modifier. The druid adds +1/2 to the number of uses per day of that domain power. In addition, a druid that selects this bonus at 1st level can choose the Darkness domain with her natural bond ability.[^0]
::h3[Archetypes]{jl}

::archetype[Aerie Protector]{c=druid r="Weapon/Armor Proficiencies; Nature Bond; Wild Empathy; Woodland Stride; Resist Nature's Lure; Wild Shape; Venom Immunity" e="The aerie protector guards nature's nests and lairs in the highest mountains, and she is only ever truly at ease when under vast expanses of open sky."}

::archetype[Ancient Guardian]{c=druid r="Weapon Proficiencies; Nature Bond; Wild Empathy; Trackless Step; Woodland Stride; Spontaneous Casting; Resist Nature's Lure; A Thousand Faces" e="Ancient guardians revere nature and draw power from its divine energies, but also protect the customs, histories, and lore of their tribe and race."}
::archetype[Ape Shaman]{c=druid r="A Thousand Faces; Venom Immunity" e="A shaman with this totem calls upon the mighty ape, a peaceful but powerful simian whose strength is beyond compare."}

::archetype[Aquatic Druid]{c=druid r="Woodland Stride; Trackless Step; Resist Nature's Lure; Venom Immunity; A Thousand Faces" e="Shepherds of the lakes and seas, aquatic druids guard ecosystems ranging from shallows streams to deep ocean trenches, ministering to their residents and communing with the tides."}

::archetype[Arctic Druid]{c=druid r="Woodland Stride; Trackless Step; Resist Nature's Lure; Venom Immunity; A Thousand Faces" e="An arctic druid watches over the stark landscape of the far frozen reaches of the world."}
::archetype[Ashvawg Tamer]{c=druid r="Resist Nature's Lure, one less Wild Shape/day, Venom Immunity" e="Ashvawg Tamers are druids who have chosen to bond with a strange and monstrous creature."}
::archetype[Bat Shaman]{c=druid r="A Thousand Faces; Venom Immunity" e="The bat shaman's totem is the agile bat, flitting and turning with incredible speed through even the most convoluted mazes."}

::archetype[Bear Shaman]{c=druid r="A Thousand Faces; Venom Immunity" e="A shaman with this focus calls upon the mighty bear, titan of the woodlands and mountains, a paragon of strength and ferocity, and yet also a quiet protector rich in wisdom."}

::archetype[Blight Druid]{c=druid r="Wild Empathy; Trackless Step; Resist Nature's Lure; Venom Immunity; A Thousand Faces" e="The devoted servants of nature corrupted, ruined, and destroyed, blight druids are the caretakers of lands ravaged by natural disaster."}
::archetype[Boar Shaman]{c=druid r="A Thousand Faces; Venom Immunity" e="A boar shaman chooses the stolid and ferocious boar as her totem."}

::archetype[Cave Druid]{c=druid r="Nature Sense; Woodland Stride; Trackless Step; Resist Nature's Lure" e="A few druids seek to preserve the hidden underdark and purge it of the fell horrors that creep up from below."}
::archetype[Death Druid]{c=druid r="Nature Bond; Wild Shape; Resist Nature's Lure; Venom Immunity" e="Some druids believe that birth and death are the most important parts of the natural cycle."}

::archetype[Defender of the True World]{c=druid r="Class Skills; Wild Empathy; Trackless Step; Resist Nature's Lure; Venom Immunity; A Thousand Faces" e="Some druids specialize in protecting Golarion from the threat of First World inhabitants."}

::archetype[Desert Druid]{c=druid r="Woodland Stride; Trackless Step; Resist Nature's Lure; Venom Immunity; A Thousand Faces" e="Desert druids come to the desert to pay homage, protect and maintain the few habitable locales, and witness nature's majesty in all its burning, merciless glory."}
::archetype[Devolutionist]{c=druid r="Nature Bond; Resist Nature's Lure; Venom Immunity" e="Devolutionists believe that the world would be better off without any sentient life at all."}

::archetype[Dinosaur Druid]{c=druid r="Spontaneous Casting; Nature's Bond; Wild Shape; Resist Nature's Lure" e="A dinosaur druid speaks for the spirit of prehistoric nature, even taking the form of great beasts of legend."}

::archetype[Draconic Druid]{c=druid r="Nature Bond; Wild Empathy; Woodland Stride; Venom Immunity; A Thousand Faces; Timeless Body; Nature Sense; Resist Nature's Lure" e="Some druids believe that dragons are the ultimate expression of nature and eventually transform into draconic forms."}

::archetype[Dragon Shaman]{c=druid r="A Thousand Faces; 8th-level Wild Shape; Venom Immunity" e="Your totem is the legendary dragon, fearsome and deadly yet cunning and wise, a creature born of pure magic and raw elemental fury, bound within a shell of fangs, claws, and scales that few dare to challenge."}
::archetype[Drovier]{c=druid r="Nature Bond; Wild Shape" e="Droviers view their allies and adventuring companions as a herd or pack to be protected and directed."}

::archetype[Eagle Shaman]{c=druid r="A Thousand Faces; Venom Immunity" e="A shaman with this totem calls upon the noble eagle, stern and proud, soaring high above the world with keen and pitiless eyes that miss nothing."}

::archetype[Elemental Ally]{c=druid r="Nature Bond; Wild Shape; Wild Empathy; Resist Nature's Lure" e="An elemental ally is a druid who has crafted powerful bonds of trust and friendship with one ally from each of the four primary elemental planes."}

::archetype[Feral Child]{c=druid r="Weapon/Armor Proficiency; Class Skills; Nature Bond; Trackless Step; A Thousand Faces; Wild Shape; Resist Nature's Lure; Venom Immunity; Timeless Body" e="(Human Only) Some youths, abandoned in the wilderness and then raised by animals, are so connected with their adoptive home and family that they become feral."}

::archetype[Feral Shifter]{c=druid r="Nature Bond; Venom Immunity; A Thousand Faces; Timeless Body" e="A feral shifter internalizes her communion with and mastery over animals. Instead of forming a bond with an animal companion or an aspect of nature, she alters her own essence or being as homage to the noble creatures of the wild."}

::archetype[Feyspeaker]{c=druid r="Spontaneous Casting; Wild Shape; Nature Sense; Base Attack Bonus; Class Skills; Skill Ranks; Armor Proficiency" e="Some druids hear the whispers of the natural world like fey creatures do, and learn to mimic those tantalizing murmurs in order to influence the minds of both wild beasts and civilized people."}
::archetype[Fungal Pilgrim]{c=druid r="Nature Bond; Wild Shape; Timeless Body" e="Fungal pilgrims are druids seeking transformation into a more plant-like existence."}

::archetype[Goliath Druid]{c=druid r="Class Skills; Nature Sense; Nature Bond; Wild Empathy; Resist Nature's Lure; Wild Shape; Venom Immunity; A Thousand Faces" e="Goliath druids hone their spiritual and magical connections to nature's largest creatures, especially dinosaurs, giants, and megafauna, revering these massive creatures as living relics of a primeval time when all creatures were more in harmony with nature."}

::archetype[Green Faith Initiate]{c=druid r="Trackless Step; 6th, 10th-level Wild Shape; Venom Immunity; A Thousand Faces" e="Druids who are initiated into the magic of nature as strict students of the Green Faith's traditions sometimes demonstrate abilities unique to their organization."}
::archetype[Green Scourge]{c=druid r="Spontaneous Casting; Nature Sense; Trackless Step; Resist Nature's Lure" e="Green scourges are militant druids sworn to restore natural order."}

::archetype[Halcyon Druid]{c=druid r="Nature Bond; Nature Sense; Class Skills; Wild Empathy; Spontaneous Casting; Wild Shape; Resist Nature's Lure; A Thousand Faces" e="Though they revere the natural world, halcyon druids are less focused on emulating its inhabitants. Instead, like Old-Mage Jatembe, they treat with beings from the Outer Sphere."}

::archetype[Jungle Druid]{c=druid r="Woodland Stride; Trackless Step; Resist Nature's Lure; A Thousand Faces" e="The jungle druid waches over the fecund jungles of the equatorial regions, rich in life and ancient tradition."}

::archetype[Kraken Caller]{c=druid r="Bonus Languages; Woodland Stride; Trackless Step; Resist Nature's Lure; Wild Shape; A Thousand Faces" e="Some druids draw upon power irreverently stolen from one of the most dangerous creatures of the depths: the kraken."}

::archetype[Leshy Warden]{c=druid r="Nature Bond; Wild Empathy; Spontaneous Casting; Resist Nature's Lure; Wild Shape; A Thousand Faces" e="The natural world is full of bodiless nature spirits connected to the forces of glades, springs, and individual plants. Some druids hear their call keenly and are able to effortlessly incarnate them as the miniature creatures known as leshys."}

::archetype[Life Channeler]{c=druid r="Alignment; Nature Bond; Wild Shape; Venom Immunity" e="By sacrificing the life of a sentient creature, life channelers can generate energy that guarantees fertility and prosperity for lower creatures like plants and animals."}
::archetype[Lion Shaman]{c=druid r="A Thousand Faces; Venom Immunity" e="A shaman with this totem calls upon the proud lion, imposing and majestic, the mighty leader of deadly hunters."}

::archetype[Mantella]{c=druid r="Wild Shape; Venom Immunity; Spontaneous Casting" e="(Grippli) A mantella focuses on exploring her poisonous heritage, whether or not it has manifested in her own personal biology."}

::archetype[Menhir Savant]{c=druid r="Nature Sense; Wild Empathy; Woodland Stride; Trackless Step; A Thousand Faces" e="Some druids study the paths of nature's power through the nodes and ley lines that connect standing stones and megalithic circles, learning to tap into their energies."}

::archetype[Mooncaller]{c=druid r="Woodland Stride; Resist Nature's Lure; Venom Immunity; A Thousand Faces" e="A mooncaller is bound to the subtle influences of the ever-changing moon and its endless cycles from light to dark and back again."}

::archetype[Mountain Druid]{c=druid r="Woodland Stride; Trackless Step; Resist Nature's Lure; Venom Immunity; A Thousand Faces" e="As more and more of the soft, easy lands become cultivated and civilized, many druids look for refuge and solitude among the eternal peaks of the highest mountains."}

::archetype[Naga Aspirant]{c=druid r="Spontaneous Casting; Resist Nature's Lure; Wild Shape; Venom Immunity; A Thousand Faces; Timeless Body" e="(Nagaji Only) The naga aspirant follows the ancient beliefs and engages in the rituals of a druidic sect dedicated to the transcendence of her nagaji form through absolute devotion to nagas and naga gods."}

::archetype[Nature Fang]{c=druid r="Nature Sense; Wild Empathy; Woodland Stride; Wild Shape; Resist Nature's Lure; Venom Immunity" e="A nature fang is a druid who stalks and slays those who despoil nature, kill scarce animals, or introduce diseases to unprotected habitats."}

::archetype[Nature Priest]{c=druid r="Deity; Class Skills; Weapon Proficiencies; Nature Sense; Nature Bond; Resist Nature's Lure; Venom Immunity" e="Nature priests serve two masters: their deities and nature itself, and can call upon the power of both to fuel their divine abilities."}

::archetype[Nithveil Adept]{c=druid r="Nature Sense; Nature Bond; Spontaneous Casting; Resist Nature's Lure" e="Nithveil adepts learn the secret magic of the First World fey in the moving, reality-phasing city of Nithveil"}

::archetype[Pack Lord]{c=druid r="Nature Bond; 6th-level Wild Shape" e="Some druids bond with many animal companions rather than just one, achieving a level of communion rare even in druidic circles and leading their pack brothers and pack sisters with total authority."}

::archetype[Plains Druid]{c=druid r="Woodland Stride; Trackless Step; Resist Nature's Lure; Venom Immunity; A Thousand Faces" e="Out upon the wide and rolling prairies and savannahs, plains druids stand guard over the grasslands."}

::archetype[Planar Extremist]{c=druid r="Alignment; Spells; Nature's Bond; Spontaneous Casting; Resist Nature's Lure; Wild Shape" e="(Ex-druid archetype) Planar extremists are those druids that have lost their neutral stance and find themselves gravitating toward one of the four most extreme alignments"}

::archetype[Progenitor]{c=druid r="Spontaneous Casting; Nature Bond; Wild Shape" e="Progenitors are druids who tap into the vibrant power of the First World to propagate natural and supernatural wonders."}

::archetype[Reincarnated Druid]{c=druid r="Woodland Stride; Resist Nature's Lure; Venom Immunity; Timeless Body" e="Spun off into the endless circle of life, an incarnate druid is an embodiment of nature's eternal renewal."}
::archetype[Restorer]{c=druid r="Nature Sense; Spontaneous Casting; Resist Nature's Lure; A Thousand Faces" e="Restorers carefully tend to the health of both the environment and creatures in it."}

::archetype[River Druid]{c=druid r="Class Skills; Nature Sense; Woodland Stride; Trackless Step; Resist Nature's Lure; Wild Shape; Timeless Body" e="These druids are the guardians of rivers and the creatures that depend on them."}

::archetype[Road Keeper]{c=druid r="Spontaneous Casting; Nature Bond; Wild Empathy; Woodland Stride; Resist Nature's Lure; Wild Shape" e="Guardians of the lonely paths between cities, road keepers protect the wild from travelers, and travelers from the wild."}

::archetype[Rot Warden]{c=druid r="Spontaneous Casting; Nature Bond; Wild Empathy; Trackless Step; Venom Immunity; Resist Nature's Lure; Wild Shape" e="Harbingers of decay, rot wardens live in damp lands where insects and rot break down the dead to feed the living."}

::archetype[Saurian Shaman]{c=druid r="Venom Immunity" e="A shaman with this focus calls upon the primeval dinosaur, the archaic terror that lingers as a hungering, atavistic stranger at the fringes of the ecosystem, a destroyer and despoiler whose coming other animals dread."}

::archetype[Season Keeper]{c=druid r="Nature Bond; Trackless Step; Venom Immunity; Timeless Body; Wild Shape" e="(Triaxian Only) Imbued with the primeval potency of their planet's seasons, those druids known as season keepers guide Triaxian communities through the stark transition from summer to winter (and vice versa)."}

::archetype[Season Sage]{c=druid r="Wild Shape" e="(Gathlain) Season sages wander as the seasons change, turning the leaves and transforming nature as they pass and taking pleasure in the transition between seasons."}

::archetype[Serpent Shaman]{c=druid r="A Thousand Faces; Venom Immunity" e="A shaman with this totem calls upon the cunning serpent, the stealthy deceiver who draws the weak minded in and strikes while they are unaware."}
::archetype[Shark Shaman]{c=druid r="A Thousand Faces; Venom Immunity" e="Some druids emulate the deadly shark, a remorseless hunter that marine dwellers dread."}

::archetype[Skinshaper]{c=druid r="Class Skills; Wild Shape; A Thousand Faces" e="Using the same primal energy with which other druids assume animal forms, skinshapers can imitate some of the most dangerous beasts of all: humans and other humanoids."}

::archetype[Sky Druid]{c=druid r="Weapon/Armor Proficiency; Nature Bond; Woodland Stride; Resist Nature's Lure; Trackless Step; Wild Shape; Venom Immunity; A Thousand Faces" e="(Sylph Only) Some druids develop ties not to a particular landscape, but instead to the endless blue expanse of the skies."}

::archetype[Storm Druid]{c=druid r="Spontaneous Casting; Woodland Stride; Trackless Step; Resist Nature's Lure; Venom Immunity; A Thousand Faces" e="While most druids focus their attention upon the rich earth and the bounty of nature that springs forth from it, the storm druid's eyes have ever been cast to the skies and the endless expanse of blue, channeling the most raw and untamed aspects of nature."}

::archetype[Sunrider]{c=druid r="Class Skills; Weapon and Armor Proficiencies; Nature Bond; Woodland Stride; Trackless Step; Resist Nature's Lure; Wild Shape" e="Sunriders help their allies, be they fellow tribe members or fellow adventurers, to survive in harsh desert terrain, while simultaneously making it even harsher for their foes."}

::archetype[Supernaturalist]{c=druid r="Weapon/Armor Proficiencies; Nature Bond; Wild Empathy; Nature Sense; Trackless Step; Resist Nature's Lure; Wild Shape" e="Supernaturalists wholly embrace paranormal phenomena as extensions of nature."}

::archetype[Survivor]{c=druid r="Class Skills; Weapon/Armor Proficiencies; Spellcasting; Nature Bond; Resist Nature's Lure; Wild Shape; Venom Immunity" e="The survivor embodies the neutral aspect of the natural world and eschews druidic magic in favor of more pragmatic and worldly survival methods."}

::archetype[Swamp Druid]{c=druid r="Woodland Stride; Trackless Step; Resist Nature's Lure; A Thousand Faces" e="Some druids eschew pleasant glades and groves and instead seek out dank marshes, misty bogs and heaths, and trackless swamps as the place they call home and watch over with care, finding beauty and life in abundance in places few others would willingly enter."}

::archetype[Swarm Monger]{c=druid r="Nature Bond; Woodland Stride; Nature Sense; Resist Nature's Lure; 12th-level Wild Shape options" e="(Ratfolk archetype) Whereas other druids commune with nature or even the spirit of a city, swarm mongers find beauty and strength in decay, and they draw their power from fungus, disease, and their own singular will to survive."}

::archetype[Tempest Druid]{c=druid r="Alignment; Weapon and Armor Proficiencies; Nature Bond; Nature Sense; Trackless Step; Resist Nature's Lure; Venom Immunity" e="Some druids have come to worship the Eye of Abendego as an aspect of pure elemental fury, channeling its mysteries into their own magic"}

::archetype[Tempest Tamer]{c=druid r="Bonus Languages; Resist Nature's Lure; Wild Shape" e="Druids draw upon the fiercest powers of nature, and tsunamis, typhoons, and whirlpools are among the most powerful forces in the ocean."}

::archetype[Toxicologist]{c=druid r="Spontaneous Casting; Wild Empathy; Woodland Stride; Trackless Step; Resist Nature's Lure; Wild Shape" e="Toxicologists specialize in the stealthy application of insidious poisons to their targets, believing word of their foes' horrifying deaths will deter others from attempting similar desecrations of the natural world."}

::archetype[Treesinger]{c=druid r="Nature Bond; Wild Empathy; Wild Shape" e="(Elf and Vine Leshy Only) Some elves turn to the timeless growth of nature for solace, finding allies among the great trees themselves, and even leading the forest's plants into combat."}

::archetype[Troll Fury]{c=druid r="Wild Empathy; Resist Nature's Lure; Venom Immunity" e="(Troll Only) Troll furies combine a druid's love of natural balance with a single-minded devotion to the welfare of the troll tribe."}

::archetype[Undine Adept]{c=druid r="Domains; Woodland Stride; Trackless Step; Resist Nature's Lure; Wild Shape; Venom Immunity" e="(Undine Only) An undine adept dedicates herself to preserving the knowledge of the first undines and ensuring her people's ancient connections to the natural world remain undisturbed."}

::archetype[Urban Druid]{c=druid r="Spontaneous Casting; Woodland Stride; Trackless Step; Resist Nature's Lure; Venom Immunity" e="While many druids keep to the wilderness, some make their way within settlements, communing with the animals and vermin who live there and speaking for the nature that runs rampant in civilization's very cradle."}

::archetype[Urushiol]{c=druid r="Nature Bond; Resist Nature's Lure" e="When an urushiol druid forms a bond with nature, it takes a vastly different form than for most druids. His body becomes increasingly toxic, allowing him to secrete a deadly poison through his pores."}
::archetype[Wild Whisperer]{c=druid r="Woodland Stride; Trackless Step; Resist Nature's Lure; Wild Shape" e="A wild whisperer is an expert at studying, predicting, and explaining animal behavior."}

::archetype[Wolf Shaman]{c=druid r="A Thousand Faces; Venom Immunity" e="A shaman with this totem calls upon the clever wolf, capable of roaming alone yet wise enough to run with a pack when facing dangers too great for one alone."}

::archetype[World Walker]{c=druid r="Trackless Step; Resist Nature's Lure; Venom Immunity; Timeless Body" e="While all druids traverse the wilderness with ease, the world walkers take it upon themselves to travel the entire world."}

monk.name: Monk
description: ## Monk
‹SOURCE PRPG Core Rulebook/56›

For the truly exemplary, martial skill transcends the battlefield - it is a lifestyle, a doctrine, a state of mind. These warrior-artists search out methods of battle beyond swords and shields, finding weapons within themselves just as capable of crippling or killing as any blade. These monks (so called since they adhere to ancient philosophies and strict martial disciplines) elevate their bodies to become weapons of war, from battle-minded ascetics to self-taught brawlers. Monks tread the path of discipline, and those with the will to endure that path discover within themselves not what they are, but what they are meant to be.

**Role:** Monks excel at overcoming even the most daunting perils, striking where it's least expected, and taking advantage of enemy vulnerabilities. Fleet of foot and skilled in combat, monks can navigate any battlefield with ease, aiding allies wherever they are needed most.
::class{al="Any lawful" lg ln le hd=8 wealth=1 skp=4 acro climb craft ea intm kh kr per perf prof ride sm stl swim}

These bonuses to AC apply even against touch attacks or when you are ‹rule/flat-footed›." imp4="You gain an additional bonus to your AC and CMD equal to ‹misc/one-fourth› of your monk level." special="You lose these bonuses when you are immobilized or ‹misc/helpless›, when you wear any armor, when you carry a shield, or when you carry a medium or heavy load."}

:::ab{jl="Flurry of Blows" title="Flurry of Blows (Ex)" l=1 icon=melee action=Ability imp8="You can make two additional attacks when you use flurry of blows, as if using ‹feat/Improved Two-Weapon Fighting› (even if you do not meet the prerequisites for the feat)." imp15="You can make three additional attacks when you use flurry of blows, as if using ‹feat/Greater Two-Weapon Fighting› (even if you do not meet the prerequisites for the feat)."}

You can make a flurry of blows as a full-attack action. When doing so you may make one additional attack using any combination of unarmed strikes or attacks with a special monk weapon (kama, nunchaku, quarterstaff, sai, shuriken, and siangham) as if using the ‹feat/Two-Weapon Fighting› feat (even if you do not meet the prerequisites for the feat). For the purpose of these attacks, your base attack bonus from your monk class levels is equal to your monk level. For all other purposes, such as qualifying for a feat or a prestige class, you use your normal base attack bonus.

You apply your full Strength bonus to your damage rolls for all successful attacks made with flurry of blows, whether the attacks are made with an off-hand or with a weapon wielded in both hands. You may substitute ‹rule/disarm›, ‹rule/sunder›, and ‹rule/trip› combat maneuvers for unarmed attacks as part of a flurry of blows. You cannot use any weapon other than an unarmed strike or a special monk weapon as part of a flurry of blows. You cannot use natural weapons (if you have any) as part of a flurry of blows, nor can you make natural attacks in addition to your flurry of blows attacks.

:::ab{jl=strip title="Unarmed Strike" l=1 icon=melee action=Ability special="Usually your unarmed strikes deal lethal damage, but you can choose to deal nonlethal damage instead with no penalty on your attack roll. You have the same choice to deal lethal or nonlethal damage while grappling. Your unarmed strike is treated as both a manufactured weapon and a natural weapon for the purpose of spells and effects that enhance or improve either manufactured weapons or natural weapons." imp4="Your unarmed strike now deals 1d6 (Small), 1d8 (Medium), or 2d6 (Large) damage." imp8="Your unarmed strike now deals 1d8 (Small), 1d10 (Medium), or 2d8 (Large) damage." imp12="Your unarmed strike now deals 1d10 (Small), 2d6 (Medium), or 3d6 (Large) damage." imp16="Your unarmed strike now deals 2d6 (Small), 2d8 (Medium), or 3d8 (Large) damage." imp20="Your unarmed strike now deals 2d8 (Small), 2d10 (Medium), or 4d8 (Large) damage."}

You gain ‹feat/Improved Unarmed Strike› as a bonus feat. Your attacks may be with fist, elbows, knees, and feet. This means that you may make unarmed strikes with your hands full. There is no such thing as an off-hand attack for your unarmed strikes, so you apply your full Strength bonus on damage rolls for all your unarmed strikes.

You also deal more damage with your unarmed strikes than a normal person would. The unarmed damage values listed on the table above are for Medium monks only. If you are Small, you deal 1d4 damage, and if you are Large, you deal 1d8 damage.
:::aside

@HL[Vows:]{jl=strip} A monk can discipline his body to by upholding the strict tenets of a vow. By adhering to his ‹ability/vow's› tenets, the monk's ki pool increases by the amount listed in the vow's description. Every vow comes with a penalty or limitation to offset this increase in ki. A monk can take a vow at any level, and can even take multiple vows over his career, but each vow does not add to his ki pool until he gains a ki pool as a class feature (usually at 4th level).
A monk who takes a vow never gains the *still mind* class feature, even if he later abandons all his vows.

::altCapstone[Old Master (Ex)]{kiSage oldDogNewTricks capstone="At 20th level, the monk has reached the highest levels of his martial arts school. The monk gains one additional attack at his highest base attack bonus when using flurry of blows, and his dodge bonus to AC increases by 2."}
::h3[Favored Class Bonuses]{jl}
:::div{className="hanging striped"}

**Catfolk:** Add 1/2 to the monk's damage rolls with claw attacks and claw blades. A monk who selects this bonus at 1st level also treats claw blades as a monk weapon. If he is an unchained monk, he can use his style strikes with unarmed strike or claw blade attacks.[^A]
**Duskwalker:** Add 1/4 point to the monk's ki pool.[^B]
**Dwarf:** Reduce the Hardness of any object made of clay, stone, or metal by 1 whenever the object is struck by the monk's unarmed strike (minimum of 0).[^C]

**Elf:** Add +1 to the monk's base speed. In combat this option has no effect unless the monk has selected it five times (or another increment of five). This bonus stacks with the monk's fast movement class feature and applies under the same conditions as that feature.[^D]
**Fetchling:** Add a +1/2 bonus on Escape Artist and Stealth checks attempted while in ‹rule/dim light› or darkness.[^E]
**Gillman:** Add 1 foot to the monk's swim speed. In combat, this option has no effect unless the monk has selected it five times (or another increment of five).[^F]
**Gnome:** Add +1 to the monk's Acrobatics check bonus gained by spending a point from his *ki* pool. A monk must be at least 5th level to select this benefit.[^G]
**Half-elf:** Add +1/2 on Escape Artist checks and on Acrobatics checks to cross narrow surfaces.[^H]
**Halfling:** Add +1 to the monk's CMD when resisting a grapple and +1/2 to the number of stunning attacks he can attempt per day.[^I] [^J]
**Halfling:** Gain a +1/4 bonus to CMD against trip and on damage rolls against prone targets that add the monk's Strength modifier.[^K]
**Half-orc:** Add +1 to the monk's CMD when resisting a grapple and +1/2 to the number of stunning attacks he can attempt per day.[^L]
**Hobgoblin:** Add a +1/4 bonus on combat maneuver checks made to grapple or trip.[^M]
**Human:** Add +1/4 point to the monk's *ki* pool.[^N] [^O]
**Kitsune:** Gain 1/6 of a new Magical Tail feat. Any kitsune character can choose this bonus upon gaining a level in her favored class.[^P]
**Kobold:** Add +1/3 to the monk's AC bonus class ability.[^Q]
**Locathah:** Add 1/4 point to the monk's ki pool.[^R]
**Merfolk:** Add a +1/4 bonus on combat maneuver checks to grapple or trip.[^S]
**Nagaji:** Add +1/4 point to the monk's *ki* pool.[^T]
**Oread:** Add +1/3 on critical hit confirmation rolls made with unarmed strikes (maximum bonus of +5). This bonus does not stack with ‹feat/Critical Focus›.[^U]

**Ratfolk:** Add +1 feet to the speed the monk can move while making a Stealth check without taking a penalty. This has no effect unless the monk has selected this reward five times (or another increment of five). This does not allow the monk to use Stealth while running or charging.[^V]
**Samsaran:** Add a +1/2 bonus on the monk's saving throws to resist death attacks.[^W]
**Strix:** Add +1/4 point to the monk's *ki* pool.[^X]
**Suli:** Add +1/2 point of damage to elemental assault.[^Y]
**Tengu:** Add +1/4 point to the monk's ki pool.[^Z]
**Undine:** Add +1 to the monk's CMD when resisting a grapple and +1/3 to the number of stunning attacks he can attempt per day.[^1]
**Vanara:** Add a +1 bonus on Acrobatics checks made to jump.[^2]
**Wayang:** Add 1/6 to the monk's AC bonus.[^3]

::archetype[Sensei]{c=monk r="Flurry of Blows; Fast Movement; Improved Evasion; Evasion; 2nd, 6th, 12th, 18th-level Bonus Feats" e="The sensei is a revered teacher who imparts lessons on the oneness of mind, body, and spirit, along with occasional correction that is subtle and swift."}

::archetype[Serpent-Fire Adept]{c=monk r="Class Skills; Stunning Fist; 1st, 2nd, 6th, 10th, 14th, and 18th-level Bonus Feats; Slow Fall; High Jump; Wholeness of Body" e="A serpent-fire adept embraces the secrets of her chakras and mastery of their energetic flow."}

::archetype[Sin Monk]{c=monk r="Ki Pool; High Jump; Wholeness of Body; Abundant Step; Empty Body; Perfect Self" e="(Ex-monk archetype) Martial experts who have allowed such passions to lead them astray may find themselves gaining powers drawn from their newly embraced sins."}

::archetype[Softstrike Monk]{c=monk r="Weapon Proficiency; Unarmed Strike; 6th, 10th-level Bonus Feats; Wholeness of Body; Quivering Palm; Empty Body" e="Softstrike monks employ a nonlethal but effective form of combat."}

::archetype[Sohei]{c=monk r="Weapon/Armor Proficiency; Stunning Fist; Fast Movement; Increased Unarmed Damage; Slow Fall; Abundant Step; Purity of Body; Diamond Body; Quivering Palm; Timeless Body; Tongue of Sun and Moon" e="The sohei are masters of the horse and the hunt."}

::archetype[Soul Shepherd]{c=monk r="Stunning Fist; Evasion; Improved Evasion; Abundant Step; Quivering Palm" e="Whether studying at the feet of psychopomps or merely imitating the creatures' philosophy and methods, soul shepherds believe in safeguarding the Cycle of Souls."}

::archetype[Spirit Master]{c=monk r="Still Mind; Maneuver Training; Purity of Body; Wholeness of Body; Quivering Palm; Empty Body; Perfect Self" e="Spirit masters are monks who specialize in combating the undead, laying their corpses to rest, and sending their souls to final judgment."}

::archetype[Student of Stone]{c=monk r="Evasion; Fast Movement; Bonus Feat; High Jump; Improved Evasion; Abundant Step" e="(Oread Only) By following the path of the stone, students of stone give up much of monks' mobility in favor of sheer resilience."}

::archetype[Terra-Cotta Monk]{c=monk r="Class Skills; Evasion; High Jump; Improved Evasion; Abundant Step; Quivering Palm; Empty Body" e="Possessing a natural affinity for all things earthen, terracotta monks are experts at turning the stone around them against their enemies."}

::archetype[Tetori]{c=monk r="Bonus Feats; Flurry of Blows; Slow Fall; High Jump; Abundant Step; Improved Evasion; Timeless Body; Tongue of Sun and Moon; Diamond Soul; Empty Body" e="The style of the tetori is that of the majestic wrestler - a warrior with a dizzying array of grabs, holds, and locks with which to bewilder and incapacitate his foes."}

::archetype[Treetop Monk]{c=monk r="Still Mind; Purity of Body; Abundant Step" e="(Vanara Only) While many vanaras follow traditional monastic training and traditions, others learn to blend exotic combat and the mysterious forces of ki with the natural world, allowing them to move through trees and overgrowth to deliver devastating attacks."}

::archetype[Underfoot Adept]{c=monk r="1st-level Bonus Feat; Stunning Fist; High Jump" e="(Halfling Only) An underfoot adept turns his diminutive stature and unorthodox footwork into a powerful weapon."}

::archetype[Wanderer]{c=monk r="Class Skills; 1st-level Bonus Feat; Still Mind; Slow Fall; High Jump; Wholeness of Body; Abundant Step; Diamond Soul" e="(Human Only) Some monks wander the world in humility to learn and to share wisdom and philosophy from their teachers with those they meet, often aiding those who are in need."}

::archetype[Wasteland Meditant]{c=monk r="Stunning Fist; Evasion; Slow Fall; Improved Evasion; Quivering Palm" e="Some monks journey to remote and isolated corners of the world to find serenity and a heightened sense of awareness."}

::archetype[Water Dancer]{c=monk r="Flurry of Blows; Stunning Fist; Quivering Palm; 1st, 2nd, 6th, 10th, 14th, and 18th-level Bonus Feats; Unarmed Strike; Evasion; Slow Fall; Improved Evasion; Abundant Step" e="Water dancers derive their martial training from ancient nereid traditions jealously guarded by these enigmatic fey."}

::archetype[Weapon Adept]{c=monk r="Stunning Fist; Evasion; Improved Evasion; Timeless Body; Perfect Self" e="While all monks train in both unarmed combat and with weapons, the weapon adept seeks to become one with his weapons, transforming them into perfect extensions of his own body."}

::archetype[Wildcat]{c=monk r="Still Mind; Ki Pool; High Jump; Slow Fall; Improved Evasion; Abundant Step; Diamond Soul; Empty Body" e="A wildcat is a student of the school of hard knocks, who dedicates himself to learning how to take down foes by any means necessary."}
::archetype[Windstep Master]{c=monk r="Stunning Fist; Slow Fall; Ki Pool" e="Most monks are agile and fleet of foot, but few can rival a windstep master's lightness of step."}

::archetype[Zen Archer]{c=monk r="Stunning Fist; Ki Pool; Evasion; Maneuver Training; Still Mind; Purity of Body; Improved Evasion; Diamond Body; Tongue of the Sun and the Moon" e="The zen archer takes a weapon most other monks eschew and seeks perfection in the pull of a taut bowstring, the flex of a bow's limbs, and the flight of an arrow fired true."}
